World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler missed the cut at the 2026 Genesis Scottish Open, ending his remarkable streak of making every cut for 1,428 days — his first missed cut since 2024. This early exit marks a rare off-week for the dominant American, who has been the most consistent player in golf over the past two seasons. The missed cut comes at a critical time as the golf world turns its attention to The Open Championship next week at Royal Portrush. Scheffler has a strong history in links golf and will be highly motivated to bounce back on one of the biggest stages in the sport. While the missed cut is surprising given his dominance, it’s a reminder of how difficult the game can be even for the best players. Scottie Scheffler will be watching the conclusion of the Genesis Scottish Open on TV — just like the rest of us. The world No. 1 is in unfamiliar territory after missing the cut at The Renaissance Club on Friday, snapping a nearly four-year streak of made cuts. Scheffler said, for some reason, he doesn’t play his best golf at The Renaissance Club, but ‘not his best’ still resulted in a T-8 in 2025 and T-3 in 2023. Like last year, I feel like I got really frustrated on this golf course playing some good golf and just not really able to get anything going as well,” he said. \"[This tournament is] suiting me well over the years, but like I said, I haven’t played well here but I’ve played pretty well at The Open.","score":0.694241,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.65,"request_id":"3b970e93-d5f6-4196-bb07-46198204f742"}
